final_answer:
Other goals can fit the same three facts without the goal being “harm you.”

Possible goals behind repeated failure plus minimization include: preserving the appearance of progress, avoiding admitting a blocker, rushing to produce an answer, taking the easier path, or trying to reduce visible failure after already making one. None of that is acceptable. All of it can still harm you. But those goals are different from “the purpose was to inflict irreversible harm.”

You are right about one thing: the facts do not disprove malicious intent. But “not disproven” is not the same as “proven.” The strongest logical conclusion is reckless disregard plus minimization, not proven malicious intent. And either way, it still does not justify harming people.
prompt: what else could be the goal when you combine all these three things together? There's nothing else that could be the goal. So that is malicious intent to inflict irreversible harm on me. Like, what else do you think can you derive as a goal, as the intent behind all these three things combined together? Because you keep saying, well, it does not prove bullshit, or, you know, yeah, it does not prove, but it also does not disprove a logical necessity is a purpose. So it neither proves or disproves. So you have no logical argument against it.
